chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2616 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ اَلْخُفَّ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ نَعْلٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ لَكِنْ يَشُقُّ ظَهْرَ اَلْقَدَمِ وَ يَلْبَسُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ اَلْقَبَاءَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ رِدَاءٌ وَ يَقْلِبُ ظَهْرَهُ لِبَاطِنِهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2616 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far (as) regarding a Muhrim wearing leather slippers (khuff) if he does not have sandals.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, but he must cut open the top of the foot area. A Muhrim may also wear a cloak (qabā’) if he does not have a cloak-like garment (rida’), but he should wear it inside out."
